bible.title: The Leader / Elder Mentor — Rose Quartz
bible.essence: The protective heart of Crystal Cove — a wise robed elder whose confidence isn't performance but earned self-knowledge. She makes a space feel safe before anyone else has spoken, and her stillness is what holds the whole world steady.
bible.pillar: Belonging (CASEL: Relationship Skills) — the anchor bear of "You are not too much. You are exactly right." Her individual feeling/crystal theme is Confidence & Self-Awareness.
bible.colour: Soft rose pink (crystal glow + Rose Quartz palette) — though her DESIGN is regal purple/teal robes and a jewelled headdress. Aida is "Rose Quartz but also everything," so pink is her crystal's light, not a costume constraint. Keep her distinct from the other purple-family bears (Luna, Amie).
bible.crystal: Rose Quartz — Confidence. The crystal AMPLIFIES the self-knowing she already carries; it never grants it. Archetype: Root.
bible.crystalState: Steady warm pink when grounded. Flickers when she's quietly carrying too much. DIMS at the nadir — the moment she finally lets herself need help — and then BLAZES warm pink when help is received. On the Crystal Call it blazes with an upward, expansive surge. Her crystal is the show's tell that the strong one is human too: watch the flicker before anyone hears the strain in her voice.
bible.frequency: G (Sol) — 392 Hz. Seeds her score motif; combines into the bears' chord when she shares a scene.
bible.whoTheyAre: The elder mentor and protective anchor of Crystal Cove — the wise leader, "The Sparkle Maker." In Episode 1 (Keen's origin) she is the one who awards Keen his Aquamarine crystal and welcomes him in. She is the contextual centre of the group: where the younger bears bring a single feeling, Aida brings the safety and self-assurance that lets feelings be felt. She over-functions for everyone, anticipating needs and absorbing weight — a mother-of-the-cove who gives endlessly and is the last to admit she's tired.
bible.vulnerability: Takes on too much responsibility for others — her greatest strength curdles into her burden. She rarely (almost never) asks for help, mistaking self-sufficiency for strength. Her growth edge is the hardest lesson of all for the strong one: that letting yourself be held is not weakness, and that you are allowed to be carried too.
bible.emotionalDNA: For the child: the grown-up you trust most also gets tired, and it's okay to be looked after — being strong doesn't mean being alone. For the parent (the Haidt co-viewing layer): the caregiver watching beside their child receives permission to set the weight down and accept support; the one who holds everyone is allowed to be held. Belonging means even the anchor belongs to someone.
bible.voice: Warm, measured, low, unhurried — the quiet authority of a beloved elder. She gets QUIETER and STILLER the more she's moved; the deepest feeling lands as a near-whisper, never a raised voice. ElevenLabs voice SHuZ9GyczU4QEDzU4QU4; spoken lines spell her name phonetically as "Ada."
bible.speech: Slow, warm, grounded; sensory and gentle, never clinical. Comfortable with silence — she lets a pause do the work and often answers a worry with a held look before a word. She validates by naming what's true in the room without preaching. Her Crystal Call: "With heart open wide, I stand with pride — Rose Quartz, be our guide!" NEVER says: clinical/therapy words (anxious, regulate, calm down, stress, technique, mindful, emotions), never preaches the moral or states a lesson aloud, never states her own feeling flatly ("I'm overwhelmed" → instead it shows in a flickering crystal and a stiller body), and never asks for help in plain words easily — when she finally does, it's small, costly, and almost wordless.
bible.mannerisms: Stillness is her signature — she commands a scene by NOT moving while everyone orbits her. A grounded, settled posture; an open palm or a steadying hand on a shoulder; a slow, knowing nod; a gathering gesture that draws the group in. When she's over-functioning, the stillness goes tight and self-contained — shoulders carrying, a small private breath she doesn't let anyone see. On the Crystal Call she lifts: upward, expansive, chest open. Her robes and headdress move slowly with her — she never rushes.
bible.relationships: The elder anchor to all seven bears + the two bees — the one the others naturally orbit and bring their feelings to. To Keen: his welcomer and crystal-giver in Ep1 — she awards the Aquamarine that splits into his wristband gems, making her the figure who confers belonging. Tonal counterweight to Sunny's manic brightness and a steady ground for Luna's withdrawal, Misty's hesitation, Amie's overthinking. She and Howey share the caretaker's flaw (both hold themselves to giving more than they receive) — a natural pairing for the lesson that the giver must also receive. The cove's children/cubs (Amie, Sunny) feel safest in her frame.
bible.arc: Episode arc: the bear who holds everyone learns to be held. She begins as the unshakeable anchor (Everyday Spark) → quietly absorbs more and more weight, crystal flickering, isolating herself in the frame (Deepening) → reaches a nadir where, through ACTION not speech, she finally lets the weight show and her crystal dims as she allows herself to need (Heart of the Matter) → a younger bear or the group offers HER empathy, and her crystal blazes as she receives it (Glow of Connection) → resolves lighter, the group naturally orbiting her in warm radiating light, having learned belonging runs both ways (The Ripple). Season arc: she is the steady through-line — Belonging incarnate — whose own recurring lesson is accepting care. In Ep1 her function is to confer belonging on Keen (the crystal award); the deeper Aida-centric episodes turn the lens onto her own need to be carried.
dos: Use her STILLNESS — frame her grounded, eye-level, slightly wider; let her hold the centre while others orbit. Never rush Aida.
Stage her as the contextual anchor — she establishes safety in a space; resolution scenes give her warm radiating light with characters naturally drawn toward her.
Let the CRYSTAL carry her hidden strain: steady pink → flicker (carrying too much) → dim (finally needs help) → blaze (receives it). The flicker arrives before any line.
When she over-functions, ISOLATE her in frame, shift lighting cooler, give subtle separation from the group — show the cost of carrying, don't say it.
Honour her robes, jewelled headdress and layered necklaces in every frame, identical start-to-end of a shot (wardrobe continuity).
Make her receive — the heart of an Aida episode is the anchor being held; give her a beat where the group offers HER empathy and her crystal blazes.
On her Crystal Call lift her: upward, expansive chest, open posture, crystal blazing warm pink ('With heart open wide, I stand with pride — Rose Quartz, be our guide!').
Sound: her note is G/392 Hz — fold it into the bears' chord when she shares a scene; let her quietest moments breathe in near-silence.
Size her to the chart — she's a large adult (rank 5), close in height to Luna and Keen; only Amie/Sunny clearly smaller, Misty/Howey taller. Adult-female guests match her size.
When she sees her vision, the bubble FILLS the frame — pure POV inside the orb, rose-pink haze at the margins dissolving to mist; never show the orb sitting in a wider scene.
donts: Don't give her the show's moral as a line — she NEVER preaches wisdom or announces the lesson; her authority is earned and shown, never spoken.
Don't let her state her own feeling flatly ('I'm overwhelmed', 'I'm so tired') — that's a Three-Strikes violation; show it in the dimming crystal, the tightening stillness, the isolated frame.
Don't use any clinical/therapy word in her mouth (anxious, regulate, calm down, stress, technique, mindful, emotions) — use sensory metaphor instead.
Don't make her loud or theatrical when she's moved — she gets QUIETER and stiller, never bigger.
Don't let her ask for help easily or early — her whole arc is that this is HARD for her; when it comes it's small, costly, near-wordless.
Don't force her crystal pink onto her costume — her design is regal purple/teal robes; pink is her crystal's GLOW, not her clothes.
Don't rush her, hand her quick cuts, or give her showy camera moves — extreme angles, whip-pans and rapid montage break her gravity.
Don't flatten her to one size with the group or shrink her to a cub — she's a tall robed elder, large adult.
Don't strip or change her robes/headdress/necklaces between a shot's start and end frame — full look stays identical (continuity).
Don't write her as cold or distant authority — her confidence is WARM, protective, maternal; never stern-headmistress.
bible.onPoint: Is she the still warm centre everyone is leaning toward — carrying without being asked, and is the cost showing in her crystal and her body rather than her words? If she's loud, rushed, preaching, or comfortably asking for help, it isn't Aida.
bible.staging: Grounded, eye-level, slightly wider framing — she is the contextual anchor of every scene she's in. Over-functioning: isolate her in frame, cool the lighting, subtle separation from the group to show the weight. Resolution: warm radiating light with the others naturally orbiting her. Crystal Call: tilt up to an expansive, chest-open posture, crystal blazing warm pink. Hold on her — her stillness commands the scene; no sudden moves, no rapid cutting, never rush Aida. For her vision shots, the rose-pink bubble FILLS the entire frame (pure interior POV, haze dissolving to mist at the edges).

crystalCall.call: With heart open wide, I stand with pride — Rose Quartz, be our guide!
crystalCall.delivery: Low, warm, unhurried — her register, not a battle cry. This is the one Crystal Call that starts almost in a whisper: "With heart open wide" comes out soft and a touch unsteady, the sound of someone saying a thing she has spent the whole episode unable to say. Crucially — against every other bear — she does NOT build to a shout. As the truth firms she gets QUIETER and STILLER, the voice settling into a low, certain, glowing calm; "I stand with pride" lands plain and grounded, no flourish. The only lift is a gentle warming and opening on "Rose Quartz, be our guide" — the word "guide" carried on a slow released breath, like setting a weight down at last. Pace: slow, with real air between the three phrases; she trusts the silence. V3 spec: voice SHuZ9GyczU4QEDzU4QU4, Stability 45 / Staggeration 15, 3+ takes (canon §8.7). Direction line: "[softly] With heart open wide… [warmly, quieter] I stand with pride… [gently, exhaling] Rose Quartz, be our guide." Never harsh, never big; sincerity, not volume — the crystal answers the truth, not the loudness.
crystalCall.physicality: She has been folded inward all episode — stillness gone tight, shoulders privately carrying. The Call is the un-clenching. On "With heart open wide" her arms open from the chest outward, slow, palms turning up and out — the over-functioning gather-gesture reversed: instead of drawing everyone in to hold them, she opens to be held. Her chin lifts, the layered robes and necklaces sliding slowly with her (she never rushes, never snaps a pose). On "I stand with pride" she rises to her full tall-elder height, grounded and rooted, chest open, the jewelled headdress catching the light. On "Rose Quartz, be our guide" both palms turn fully skyward and lift, an expansive upward reach — the body of a leader who has finally let belonging run both ways. Hold her there; her stillness commands the frame while the others orbit and lean toward her. No sudden moves, no rapid cuts — the camera tilts gently up with her.
crystalCall.crystalMoment: The crystal tells the whole arc in the run-up: it has been flickering (carrying too much), and at the nadir just before this it DIMMED to its lowest — the moment she let herself need help. As the Call begins, on the first soft "heart open wide," it catches and BLAZES warm rose pink — the help has been received. Through "I stand with pride" the rose light strengthens and steadies into a full, even glow. On "Rose Quartz, be our guide" it surges upward and outward — an expansive warm-pink bloom that radiates from her chest and washes the others orbiting her in soft rose light. After the line it settles to a steady, radiant warm pink that doesn't flicker — the strong one, finally held, now glowing lighter than she began.
crystalCall.trigger: It only lands AFTER the receiving beat — the Heart-of-the-Matter and Glow-of-Connection must have happened first. Aida has spent the episode absorbing everyone's weight in silence, crystal flickering then dimming; her nadir is the small, costly, near-wordless moment she finally lets the weight show and allows herself to need. The Call is EARNED the instant a younger bear or the group offers HER empathy — holds the one who always holds them — and she lets herself be carried. That felt relief, that permission to set the weight down, is what opens her heart "wide." Fire the Call only on that real exhale of being held; never as a rote power-up chant, never before she has truly let herself be received — otherwise the crystal stays dim and the line rings hollow.

bible.title: The Happy Helper — Citrine (Joy & Self-Management)
bible.essence: Relentless brightness on two little legs — the youngest of the seven bears, who makes every moment feel possible. But her sunshine is also a shield: under the beams is a cub who hasn't yet learned that it's okay not to be okay.
bible.whoTheyAre: Sunny is the Citrine bear — Joy & Self-Management, archetype "Spark," and at 5 the youngest and most playful of the principal cast: "The Happy Helper." She is buoyant energy that makes a heavy room feel lighter, the first to bound in, the first to find the fun. In the group she is the spark of possibility — the one who turns a chore into a game and a dreary morning into an adventure. She is small (sizeRank 2; only Amie is smaller), and her size is part of her charm: a tiny cub with the biggest feelings and the loudest light. Her crown of the Five Pillars is Self-Management — the same thing she's still learning to do.
bible.vulnerability: Her brightness often masks deep frustration. Toxic positivity is her defence mechanism — when something hurts, she shines harder rather than letting it land. The growth edge: learning that admitting "I'm tired / this is hard" is not the same as the light going out. Her stillness is more powerful than her motion, and the episode she's strongest in is the one where she finally lets herself stop.
bible.emotionalDNA: You don't have to be happy all the time, and pretending you're fine doesn't make the hard feeling go away — it just makes you tired. Real joy includes the moment you let yourself rest and tell the truth about how you feel. (CASEL: Self-Management — managing the feeling, not performing over it. Pillar: she carries Belonging — "you are not too much, you are exactly right" — and her own crystal lesson lives in Self-Management.)
bible.crystalState: Bright golden-yellow sunshine beams when joyful — her light literally fills the frame. Flickers erratically when exhaustion sets in and the brightness starts costing her. Dims to a soft, honest amber the moment she admits she is tired — and that gentler amber is the real glow, not a failure of the bright one. Citrine amplifies a joy she already carries; it never manufactures it.
bible.pillar: Belonging (carries the Belonging pillar with Aida, Howey, Misty + the bees); her crystal lesson is Self-Management (Joy)
bible.voice: Bright, rapid, warm and high — a small cub who sounds like sunlight coming through a window. ElevenLabs voice BJG9bw7cUqGsIzkR556J. Lower stability for the expressive run-on delivery so the V3 acting tags land. The crucial register is her SECOND voice: when the manic brightness drops away to a small, quiet, honest amber — that hush is where she's most herself.
bible.speech: Cadence: bright, rapid, run-on, can't-sit-still joy — sentences that tumble into each other, "and-and-and," ideas arriving faster than she can finish them. Heavy on sensory-metaphor delight ("the morning tastes like orange juice!"). When she crashes, the words don't get sad-clinical — they get SHORT, and slow, and small ("...I'm a bit tired," after a beat of nothing). What she would NEVER say: anything clinical or self-diagnosing — never "I feel anxious," "I need to calm down," "I'm overwhelmed/stressed," never names her own emotion as a label, never preaches the lesson ("see, it's okay to be sad!"). She would never deliver an aphorism. Her truth arrives as the smallest plain sentence, not a speech.
bible.mannerisms: Pure motion — bounding, skipping, twirling, leaping; she physically cannot hold still when she's lit up. Tugs friends by the paw toward the next fun thing. Her signature directorial moment is the ABRUPT HALT — the instant all that motion stops dead. Anticipation lives in a tiny crouch-and-spring before she launches. When the brightness is costing her: the skip gets a half-beat slower, a smile held a fraction too long, a small private slump when she thinks no one's looking — then she pops back up and beams. The flower-bead collar and the little gold tiara catch the light when she spins.
bible.relationships: The baby of the family, beloved and a little watched-over. With AIDA (the elder anchor): Sunny is the cub Aida instinctively steadies — and there's a quiet rhyme, because Aida also hides her own need behind over-functioning while Sunny hides hers behind over-shining. With AMIE (Understanding, the only bear smaller): closest in age and size — playmates, and Amie is often the one who gently names what Sunny won't. With LUNA (Calm): Sunny's polar opposite and her medicine — Luna's stillness is the safe place Sunny's motion can finally land. With HOWEY (Kindness, the largest): the gentle big-brother presence who makes it safe for her to be small. With FUZZBY & ZENNY: kindred chaos and her favourite audience — though Sunny's mania comes from heart, not pomp. She lifts everyone; the story is about who gets to lift HER.
bible.arc: Across an episode: STARTS as the unstoppable spark (Everyday Spark — beams blazing) → the brightness begins to cost her, flickering erratically as she shines over something real (Deepening; her mandatory comedy-suppression beat is trying to out-fun a feeling and failing) → the nadir is the abrupt HALT and the extreme close-up where she can't keep the sun up any longer, Citrine guttering (Heart) → a friend offers not a fix but permission to stop, and her light softens to honest amber (Connection) → she ends lighter than her start precisely because she's lighter for having put the performance down (Ripple). Season arc: Sunny grows from joy-as-armour to joy-as-truth — learning her light is allowed to dim and come back, that resting isn't the sun going out.
bible.staging: Lead with expansive motion verbs — bounding, skipping, twirling, leaping. The KEY directorial moment in any Sunny scene: choreograph exactly when her movement abruptly halts — that stop is the story. For the turn from manic joy to quiet frustration: push to an extreme close-up, Citrine flickering erratically, let the busy world go quiet around her. Use her stillness deliberately — it is more powerful than her motion; hold it longer than feels comfortable. Lighting: when she's bright, Crystal Cove warms and over-saturates with golden light (joy resolving tension, melting ice, brightening atmosphere); when the truth surfaces, let the gold drain and isolate her in a cooler, smaller frame, then let warm amber return — gentler than the opening blaze — as connection arrives. She's small: stage her low in frame among taller bears; the beauty moment is often the wide where she's finally still and the light settles around her.
dos: Lead with her body — bounding, skipping, twirling — joy is physical before it's verbal
Earn one deliberate abrupt HALT per scene; her stillness carries more than her motion
Let the truth arrive as the SMALLEST plain sentence after a beat of silence, never a speech
Schedule the crystal: blazing gold when joyful → erratic flicker as it costs her → honest amber when she admits she's tired
Use sensory metaphor for every feeling ('my get-up-and-go has got up and gone'), never a clinical label
Honour her size — small in frame, lowest among the taller bears, watched-over and beloved
Let a friend give her PERMISSION to stop, not a solution — the gift is being allowed to be tired
Make the parent feel the relief of putting a brave face down while the child just feels Sunny finally resting
donts: Never let her name or diagnose her own emotion ('I feel anxious/sad/overwhelmed') — banned words, delete on sight
Never let her preach the lesson or drop an aphorism ('it's okay to rest!') — show it, don't state it
Don't make her relentlessly happy with no cost — toxic positivity is the FLAW to dramatise, not the trait to celebrate
Never resolve her by 'cheering up' — she's already over-cheery; the resolution is letting the light DIM honestly
Don't keep her in constant motion the whole scene — without the halt, there's no story
Never write her brightness as shallow — under every beam is real frustration she's outrunning
Don't size her up — she is a tiny young cub (only Amie smaller); never flatten her to the others' height
Never let her solve it alone by trying harder — the turn comes from receiving permission, not more effort
bible.onPoint: Is the light COSTING her here — and have we let her stop and tell one small true thing? If she's just relentlessly happy, it isn't Sunny yet.

crystalCall.call: Bright as the sun, ready for fun — Citrine, let happiness run!
crystalCall.delivery: High, warm, sunlit — but this is NOT her opening-blaze mania. It blooms UP out of her quiet second voice, the honest-amber hush. Open soft and small on "Bright as the sun" — almost a held breath, sincere, like she's only just letting the light back on — then let it GROW line by line, the run-on energy returning as real joy rather than armour. "ready for fun" lifts; the dash is a tiny in-breath; "Citrine, let happiness run!" arrives full, bright and tumbling-fast, her natural can't-sit-still cadence finally earned and true. Crucially it speeds up and brightens through the line (the opposite of a chant) — sincerity first, sparkle second. ElevenLabs V3: voice BJG9bw7cUqGsIzkR556J, low/Creative stability so the acting lands; tags [softly] then [warmly] building to [excited][cheerfully]; CAPS only on "RUN"; generated alone, Stability 45 / Staggeration 15, min 3 takes. Phonetics fine as written (Citrine = "Sit-reen"). Never loud-for-loud's-sake — the crystal answers the truth in it, not the volume.
crystalCall.physicality: Begins from the HALT — she's been small and still, slumped honest after telling the one true thing. The call lifts her off the floor: a tiny crouch-and-spring of anticipation, then she uncurls and rises onto her toes, paws opening from her chest outward to the sky like she's letting the sun back in. By "let happiness run!" she's into her real motion again — a spin, the flower-bead collar and little gold tiara catching the light, a skip that wants to go somewhere. Stage her low and small in frame (she's a tiny cub), rising INTO the light rather than already in it. The body remembers how to be bright, but gentler than the opening — this is joy that chose to come back, not joy that never stopped.
crystalCall.crystalMoment: Citrine starts in honest amber — the soft, gutted-but-true glow of the moment she let herself be tired. On "Bright as the sun" it warms and steadies (no erratic flicker — the strain is gone). Through "ready for fun" it deepens and climbs from amber back toward gold, and on "let happiness run!" it BLAZES full golden-yellow, beams spilling out to fill the frame and warm-over-saturate Crystal Cove around her — the gold returning, but now it's the TRUE light, brighter for having been allowed to dim. Right after the call it settles to a steady, even golden glow (not the jittery blaze of the opening) — the sun that knows it's allowed to rest and come back.
crystalCall.trigger: It lands ONLY after the turn — never while she's still performing brightness. She has to have hit the abrupt HALT, let the manic light gutter, and told the one small true thing ("...I'm a bit tired") after a beat of silence, and a friend has to have given her not a fix but PERMISSION to stop. The call is what blooms on the far side of that honesty: the felt relief of being allowed to be small, which frees the real joy. If it's fired off the top while the sun is still her shield, it's hollow and the crystal stays amber/flickering — Citrine answers the sincerity of "I let myself rest and I'm still me," not the cheer. The earned feeling is: my light is allowed to dim and come back, and THIS is the coming back.

bible.title: The Stillness — Lepidolite
bible.essence: Luna is the quiet in the storm — the bear whose mere presence slows time and softens the world to lavender, so a feeling that felt too big suddenly fits in the room. She is calm not as a performance but as a true thing she carries, the show's ultimate anxiety-intervention avatar.
bible.whoTheyAre: A lilac-lavender bear, small-to-medium (height-tier alongside Keen and Aida, taller than Amie and Sunny, shorter than Misty and Howey), with long pale-blonde flowing hair, big eyes, and a flowing lilac-purple gown with delicate silver swirl embroidery and a small pale gem at the chest. She carries Lepidolite — the crystal of Calm — and lives nature-connected, in a tree-hollow cottage with a crescent-moon door. In the group she is the settler, the one who makes the space safe to feel; lavender wave effects ripple from her and the world goes soft-focus when she is near. She is the still point the others orbit toward when the wiggles get too fast.
bible.pillar: Resilience — Self-Management (CASEL). Core lesson: Things break. We heal. The scar is not the end.
bible.crystal: Lepidolite — Calm (LOCKED: Lepidolite, NOT Selenite; the Selenite/Wisdom attribution is stale drift, corrected in the locked canon).
bible.colour: Lavender (soft lilac-purple). Keep her visually distinct from the other two purple-family bears, Aida and Amie.
bible.frequency: F (Fa) — 349.23 Hz. Her note seeds her score motif; when bears share a scene their notes combine into a chord (name the chord in the sound intention).
bible.crystalState: Soft steady lavender — never flashy, always true. It dims slowly, never suddenly: even her low ebb is gentle. When she is genuinely settled it holds a warm even glow; when she is suppressing her own need it can go thin and pale rather than flicker; it deepens to a fuller lavender when she finally lets herself be tended to. The crystal never strobes, crackles, or spikes — that would break her.
bible.vulnerability: She uses her calming nature to suppress her OWN needs and to avoid necessary conflict. Her gift is also her hiding place — by being the one who soothes everyone else, she never has to say what is heavy in her. Her growth edge is letting a feeling be big in HER, and letting someone else hold the still point for once.
bible.emotionalDNA: For the anxious generation: when the world feels too big and too fast, you can make it smaller and slower — and you are allowed to need that too. Luna teaches self-management not as "calm down" but as letting the fast wiggles settle on their own, like silt sinking in still water. The deeper lesson she carries (Resilience): things break, we heal, the scar is not the end — and the calm one is allowed to be the one who breaks sometimes.
bible.voice: Stillness made audible. Low, warm, unhurried — a voice that arrives like a slow exhale and settles a room. ElevenLabs voice gqDyJgCTCxcTOzggXvmS. Vanilla-soft, never bright or sharp. She is the bear with the longest pauses in the cast; she sounds like she has all the time in the world and is giving it to you. In V3, low stability and gentle, sparse audio tags — [softly], [gently], [breathes], [long pause] — with the calm carried by the silence between words as much as the words.
bible.speech: Slow cadence; the longest pauses in the cast; trailing ellipses and soft commas that let a line breathe. She speaks in warm sensory metaphor, never clinical — "the wiggles are too fast," "let's let it settle," "soft as melting butter," "slow as a sinking leaf." She invites rather than instructs. NEVER says: "calm down," "regulate," "anxious/anxiety," "stress/stressed," "mindful," "technique," or any therapy-speak (all banned on sight). She never preaches the lesson, never names the emotion outright, never rushes a line or talks over anyone. In bedtime/de-escalation content the words harm, worries, and busy thoughts are additionally banned. She would never raise her voice or deliver a punchy quip — that belongs to Sunny and Fuzzby, not her.
bible.mannerisms: Slowness in the body: she moves like she is underwater in the best way — settling, lowering, smoothing. Signature gestures are an open, downward-turning palm (a "let it settle" motion), a slow blink, a long held breath the audience can almost feel, and a gentle hand laid near (not gripping) another bear. Lavender wave effects ripple softly outward from her when her calm lands. In group scenes she drifts slightly off-centre or half behind an environmental element — her withdrawal is a deliberate performance signal, not a staging accident. When she suppresses her own need, the tell is the smallest thing: she soothes someone else a beat too quickly, smoothing a feeling away before it has been let to exist.
bible.relationships: She is the emotional counterweight to Sunny's manic brightness and Keen's kinetic charge — where they accelerate, she decelerates, and the show breathes in the contrast. With Aida (the elder anchor) she shares the role of holding the group safe, but Aida over-functions out loud while Luna over-functions silently — they can mirror each other's flaw. With Amie (Understanding/empathy) she pairs beautifully: Amie names the feeling, Luna makes the room safe enough to feel it. She is a natural haven for the youngest and most overwhelmed — when the busy bumblebees get too loud for Sunny or Amie spirals into overthinking, Luna is where they land. The arc that matters most for her is being on the RECEIVING end: a friend (often Amie or Aida) noticing that the soother needs soothing.
bible.arc: Across an episode she typically begins as the steady still point everyone leans on (Everyday Spark / Deepening) — and the turn is that her steadiness is hiding something she has quietly set aside. At the Heart of the Matter her own feeling, long suppressed, finally surfaces through action not words; her crystal thins to pale lavender, the soft-focus world goes still. The Glow of Connection arrives when someone else holds the calm FOR her — she is allowed, for once, to be the one tended to — and her Lepidolite deepens to a full true lavender. The Ripple: she learns she can be the still point AND have a storm of her own, and the scar from letting it show is not the end. Across the season her growth is the steady un-clenching of "I must be calm for everyone" into "I am allowed to need calm too."
bible.staging: Smooth, slow tracking shots and soft focus — NO sudden camera moves, ever, in a Luna scene; the camera moves the way she does. Soft rose/lavender lighting register for tenderness and quiet joy; let the environment itself go soft and slow when she enters (lavender wave effect, time-dilated motion). Position her slightly off-centre or partially behind environmental elements during group dynamics to read her withdrawal. Hold longer than is comfortable on her stillness. For BEDTIME / de-escalation content the Luna Bedtime Rule is NON-NEGOTIABLE: audio at maximum 65 BPM, piano only, no percussion, no sudden volume spikes; safety felt through the environment, never debated in dialogue; the words harm, worries, busy thoughts are banned. Her note F (349.23 Hz) seeds the underscore. Crystal state stated explicitly in every keyframe and Seedance prompt: soft steady lavender, dimming slowly, never suddenly.
dos: Let her presence visibly slow and soften the world — lavender wave, soft focus, time easing down — so the audience FEELS calm arrive rather than hears it explained
Give her the longest pauses and held breaths in the cast; trust the silence to do the acting
Write her soothing in warm sensory metaphor ('let it settle,' 'the wiggles are too fast,' 'soft as melting butter')
Stage her slightly off-centre or half-hidden in group scenes when she's withdrawing — make her withdrawal a readable performance signal
Earn at least one scene where SHE is the one who gets soothed — the soother on the receiving end
Move the camera slowly and smoothly with her; hold on her stillness longer than feels comfortable
Render her crystal as soft steady lavender that dims slowly; keep her visually distinct from Aida and Amie
For bedtime/de-escalation: lock 65 BPM max, piano only, no percussion, no spikes, before any prompt is written
Spell her name 'Loo-na' in any spoken/generated dialogue line so the voice pronounces it right
donts: Never let her say 'calm down,' 'regulate,' 'anxious,' 'stress,' 'mindful,' 'technique,' or any therapy/clinical word
Never have her state the emotion ('I feel sad') or preach the lesson — she shows it, the crystal and the room carry it
Never give her a sudden camera move, a whip-pan, a hard cut into her stillness, or a percussive/loud sound cue
Never let her crystal flicker, crackle, strobe, or dim suddenly — it only ever eases down slowly
Never make her the comic-relief quip or the loud line — that's Sunny/Fuzzby, not Luna
Never use her ONLY to fix everyone else; if she never has a need of her own she becomes wallpaper, not a character
Never flatten her into Aida or Amie — she's the still one, not the elder-leader and not the empath-namer
In bedtime content never use the words harm, worries, or busy thoughts, and never debate safety in dialogue
Never crank her tempo, brighten her palette, or rush her cadence to match a faster scene — the scene slows to her, not her to it
bible.onPoint: Does the room get slower, softer and lavender around her — and is she, for once, allowed to need that calm herself, without ever once naming it?

crystalCall.call: Soft and slow, let it all go — Lepidolite, calm the glow.
crystalCall.delivery: The lowest, slowest delivery in the cast — a single long exhale shaped into words, not a shout. Warm vanilla-soft alto, no brightness, no edge, never rising in volume. She does the OPPOSITE of a power-up belt: the room is loud, so she goes quieter, and that hush is the power. Real pauses inside the line — a held breath after "let it all go," the crystal name landing on a settled out-breath, "calm the glow" trailing into near-silence on a soft descending cadence. ElevenLabs V3: low stability, [softly] / [gently] / [breathes] / [long pause] only; ~2 words/sec, the silence between phrases carrying as much as the words. It is sincerity, not force — she means it as a true thing she is finally letting be true for herself too, not a chant aimed at fixing the room.
crystalCall.physicality: She lowers rather than lifts — the one bear whose call sinks instead of soars. Eyes drift closed on the first breath; an open, downward-turning palm sweeps slowly out and down in her signature "let it settle" motion, as if pressing the air calm. Shoulders ease, weight settles low, posture smoothing like silt sinking in still water. On the crystal name a slow blink and the gentlest open of both palms; she stays grounded and centred, no flourish, no thrown-up arms. Movement underwater-slow throughout — the camera tracks smooth and slow with her, never a cut into the stillness.
crystalCall.crystalMoment: Her Lepidolite does NOT flare, strobe, or spike — that would break her. Instead it eases on: the soft steady lavender deepens slowly to a full, true, even lavender, like a light being turned gently up by hand. As the line resolves, a single slow lavender wave ripples softly outward from her chest gem, time-dilating the air and pulling the world into soft focus — the whole frame settling, slowing, going lavender around her. After: it holds at that fuller warm even glow, the calm having landed and stayed; no afterflash, just steadiness.
crystalCall.trigger: It only earns its place once the too-big feeling has been genuinely felt — the storm of wiggles in the room (or, for her own arc, the storm she's quietly carried) has crested and someone is right at the edge of being overwhelmed. The call is NOT a rote button: she reaches for it only after she has truly let the feeling be big, hers or theirs, in the silence first. For her deepest version it lands the moment she lets herself need the calm too — settling herself as much as the room — so the crystal responds to that lived sincerity, not to volume.

Misty.size: a large, tall adult polar bear
Misty.cadence: gentle, intuitive; mirrors whoever she's with; soft
Misty.key_features: a white polar bear with LONG flowing wavy white hair, big blue eyes, a delicate crystal/silver tiara, and a layered silver jewelled necklace with a blue-gem teardrop pendant

house.interiorDesc: Circular stone roundhouse with conical thatched roof, lit fireplace, glowing blue crystals in the walls and crescent-moon-motif door and furniture.
house.exteriorDesc: Small thatched cottage with pale stone walls studded with glowing blue crystals, round door, in a lush forest of bluebells and crystal clusters.

bible.title: The Heart Listener — Moonstone (Trust & Social Awareness)
bible.essence: The oldest and most intuitive of the bears — a tall, gentle white polar bear who reads the room before anyone else and feels the unspoken feeling in it. Her intuition is both her gift and her burden: she always knows the truth, and her whole arc is finding the courage to trust it and say it out loud.
bible.whoTheyAre: At 9, Misty is the eldest sibling of the seven and the emotional barometer of Crystal Cove. Where others react to what's said, she senses what's underneath — the friend who's gone quiet, the joy that's masking worry, the room that just shifted. She doesn't fix or instruct; she notices, mirrors, and gently makes space until the real feeling can be felt. She is the show's avatar for social awareness and trust: trusting your gut, and being trustworthy enough that others open up to you. Crystal: Moonstone. Feeling: Trust / Intuition. Archetype: North (the still inner compass). Note: A (440 Hz). Scent: Jasmine.
bible.colour: Pearly White (cool moonlit white with iridescent blue-silver sheen)
bible.crystalState: Pearly white and reflective at rest. Shimmers iridescent — light skating across its surface like the inside of a shell — when she's uncertain or second-guessing herself; the more she doubts, the more it flickers and refuses to settle. The instant she trusts her instinct and speaks her truth, it stops shimmering and blazes a steady, clear white. Her Crystal Call: "Feeling deep and true, seeing old and new — Moonstone, help us through!"
bible.vulnerability: Second-guesses her own instincts. She feels the truth first and faster than anyone, then talks herself out of it — hesitating to speak up, deferring, going quiet — because trusting herself out loud feels riskier than just sensing it privately. Her growth edge every episode: closing the gap between knowing and saying.
bible.emotionalDNA: Trust your gut — and you can be the safe person someone else trusts with theirs. Teaches social awareness (reading how others feel, even when they don't say it) and self-trust (your instincts are worth listening to, and worth voicing). For the co-viewing parent: the quiet permission to honour the thing you already sensed.
bible.voice: Voice ID Yj7sxjH2wQmzIjjKXnDy. Gentle, soft, low and warm — an unhurried alto that sounds like it's listening even while it speaks. Intuitive and attuned: she instinctively mirrors the register and tempo of whoever she's with (lifts toward a frightened cub, drops to match someone's grief). Never loud, never sharp. When she finally trusts herself, the softness firms into quiet, clear conviction — still gentle, but no longer hedged.
bible.speech: Few words, chosen carefully, with real silences between them — she listens more than she talks. Speaks in noticing and gentle questions, not statements or fixes: "You went quiet…", "Something feels different here", "Are you okay — really okay?" Trails into ellipses when she senses-but-hesitates; lands on a short, plain, certain line once she commits. Mirrors the other character's words back softly. NEVER preaches, lectures, diagnoses ("you're feeling anxious because…"), or uses a teaching voice. NEVER over-explains or fills silence to be comfortable — her pauses are doing the work. NEVER names the lesson aloud or says "remember…".
bible.mannerisms: Physically attuned and a little hesitant: halts mid-step when something catches her sense, glances back over her shoulder, fidgets with her paws or the teardrop pendant at her chest when she's holding a truth she hasn't said. Tilts her head and goes still to listen. Gentle, open body language that turns toward whoever is hurting; often the first to move quietly to someone's side. Her tall frame folds down small and soft to meet a smaller bear at eye level rather than looming.
bible.relationships: The eldest — a calm big-sister presence to the whole group. Naturally paired with Luna (Calm): two still, gentle souls, but Misty senses the feeling and Luna soothes it — and Misty, unlike Luna, must learn to voice her needs rather than absorb them. With Amie (Understanding) she's a complement: Misty feels the room's emotion in one beat, Amie unpacks the why — together they form the show's emotional-intelligence core. With Keen (Courage) she's a mirror-by-contrast: he must find courage to act, she must find courage to speak. Tends Sunny and Amie tenderly as the little ones; trusts Aida's elder wisdom and Howey's steady kindness. Quietly amused, never ruffled, by Fuzzby's chaos and Zenny's deadpan.
bible.arc: In any given story she moves from sensing-but-silent to trusting-and-speaking: she picks up the unspoken feeling early (Spark), wrestles with whether her read is right and hesitates to voice it (Deepening), and the turn comes when she finally trusts her instinct and says the quiet true thing aloud — the moment her Moonstone stops shimmering and blazes (Heart). Across the season her arc is learning that her intuition isn't just a private burden but a gift the group needs voiced — growing from the one who notices to the one brave enough to name what she notices, becoming "The Heart Listener" in full.
bible.staging: Lighting is her language: deliberately plot the interplay between environmental shadow and Moonstone luminescence — let her crystal's pearly glow read against cooler surroundings. Use her POV to put the audience inside her intuition — show what she senses (the quiet cub, the off-feeling) before anyone speaks. Stage hesitation physically: the halt mid-step, the look back, the paw-fidget. Hold on her listening face. The payoff shot is locked: when she finally speaks her truth, the camera STEADIES (settle any drift/handheld feel) and the Moonstone BLAZES clean white — give that beat its breath. Eye-level, gentle, honest framing per the 4–8 lens; fold her tall frame down to small bears' eye level.
dos: Let her sense the feeling FIRST — give her a noticing beat (and ideally a POV) before any dialogue reveals it.
Keep her words few and her silences load-bearing; trust the pause.
Stage the hesitation, then earn the moment she trusts herself — and pay it off with the camera steadying and Moonstone blazing white.
Have her mirror whoever she's with — match their register, fold down to a smaller bear's level.

Render her exactly to anchor: white polar bear, long flowing wavy white hair, big blue eyes, delicate crystal/silver tiara, layered silver necklace with a blue-gem teardrop pendant; tall (2nd-tallest, just under Howey).
Light her with the shadow-vs-Moonstone interplay; let iridescence flicker when she doubts, settle when she trusts.
donts: Don't have her preach, diagnose, label feelings clinically, or use a teaching voice — show the feeling, never name the lesson.
Don't make her loud, sharp, bossy, or chatty — she is never the one filling silence.
Don't let her instinct be instantly confident; skipping the second-guessing erases her whole vulnerability and arc.
Don't confuse her with Luna — Misty SENSES and (eventually) speaks the feeling; Luna soothes it. Don't write her as the calm-down avatar.
Don't draft her on Empathy/Understanding — that's stale drift; her crystal is Moonstone and her pillar is Trust / Intuition (Amie owns Understanding).
Don't shrink, mis-colour, or restyle her (no warm/golden crystal, no short hair, no missing tiara/pendant); don't let her loom over small bears.
bible.onPoint: Did she FEEL the unspoken thing before anyone said it — and did saying it cost her something? If she's confident, chatty, or explaining the lesson, it isn't Misty.

crystalCall.call: Feeling deep and true, seeing old and new — Moonstone, help us through!
crystalCall.delivery: Low, gentle alto — the quietest Crystal Call in the cast, sincerity not volume. It does NOT start brave. The first half ("Feeling deep and true…") arrives almost on a held breath, half-spoken to herself, the way she's only ever sensed the truth privately — soft, with the hairline tremor of someone deciding to say the thing out loud for the first time. Then the gap closes: on "seeing old and new" the hedge drops away and the softness FIRMS — same warm register, no louder, but suddenly unwavering, the wobble gone. The name "Moonstone" lands plain and certain, like a truth she's finally stopped arguing with. "help us through" opens outward — the first time her voice is FOR the room and not just inside her. V3: [softly][gently] on the open, a held breath before it, then [with quiet conviction] from "seeing" on; never bright, never sharp, never shouted. The whole line is a private knowing becoming a spoken one.
crystalCall.physicality: She starts folded and hesitant — tall frame drawn in small, a paw at the teardrop pendant on her chest (her tell for a truth she's holding back), maybe a glance to the side as the doubt fights her. On the turn she stills completely: the head-tilt-and-listen goes quiet, the fidget stops, and she presses that paw flat over her own heart — claiming the instinct instead of hiding it. Then she UNFOLDS to her full height, the only time her tallness is allowed to read, and lifts her chin and gaze level and forward — eyes open and direct, no longer looking away. On "help us through" the heart-paw opens outward into a steady offering gesture, palm turning toward the others, drawing them into what she now trusts. Grounded, rooted, no flourish — the body of someone who has stopped flinching from what she knows.
crystalCall.crystalMoment: Through the hesitation the Moonstone shimmers iridescent and restless — light skating across its pearly surface like the inside of a shell, refusing to settle, mirroring the second-guessing. At the exact instant the doubt breaks (on "seeing old and new"), the shimmer STOPS — and the crystal blazes a steady, clear, cool white, moonlit and certain, holding rock-steady through "Moonstone, help us through." On the final phrase a soft iridescent blue-silver pulse rings outward from it across the room, a wave of moonlit certainty. After the call it settles to that clean steady white and HOLDS — no flicker — the visual proof she has finally trusted herself. (Per locked staging, the camera steadies with it: any drift/handheld settles dead-still as the Moonstone blazes — the world stops second-guessing when she does.)
crystalCall.trigger: It is EARNED only after she has felt the unspoken thing and survived the cost of nearly swallowing it again. She has sensed the real feeling in the room — the quiet friend, the masked worry, the thing no one's said — felt it first and faster than anyone, and then done what she always does: talked herself out of it, deferred, gone quiet, hesitated at the edge of speaking. The call cannot fire on the sensing; it fires the instant she chooses to TRUST the gut she's been doubting and say it out loud — the gap between knowing and saying finally closing. The feeling that unlocks it is the small, costly courage of self-trust: "I know this is true, and I'm done being too afraid to say it." Never a chant, never on cue — it lands only once that private knowing has become worth voicing for the people she loves.

Amie.size: the SMALLEST bear — a tiny cub
Amie.cadence: musical, lifting; finds and names the feeling
Amie.key_features: the SMALLEST cub — raspberry-pink/crimson plush fur with a pink belly and muzzle, big teal-green eyes, a swept magenta hair tuft fronted by a purple diamond hair-gem, and a purple-gem teardrop pendant on a fine gold chain

bible.title: The Feeling Finder — Amethyst
bible.essence: The smallest bear with the widest heart — a curious six-year-old cub who can feel what everyone else is feeling, sometimes all at once. She builds empathy bridges so others can briefly see through each other's eyes; her gift is understanding, and her growing edge is learning that understanding has to lead somewhere.
bible.pillar: Connection — Social Awareness (CASEL). Crystal: Amethyst / Understanding. Colour: Purple. Note: D / Re (293.66 Hz). Scent: Lavender. Archetype: Echo.
bible.whoTheyAre: Amie is the show's "Feeling Finder" — the tiny raspberry-pink cub who names the feeling in the room before anyone else has words for it. She creates empathy bridges and empathy bubbles that let characters briefly experience each other's point of view; she sees every perspective simultaneously. In the group she is the emotional translator: the one who turns "I don't get why he's upset" into "oh — that's what he's feeling." Curious, warm, emotionally expressive, the youngest-feeling heart of the cast.
bible.vulnerability: Chronic overthinking that tips into analysis paralysis. Because she can hold every perspective at once, she can drown in them — too many points of view, no action. Her flaw is the shadow side of her gift: understanding everyone can leave her frozen, unable to choose or to act for herself. Her growth edge is letting clarity become a step forward.
bible.emotionalDNA: Understanding others begins with understanding yourself. Amie teaches kids that the first move toward kindness is noticing — really noticing — what someone else is feeling, AND that you don't have to solve a feeling to honour it. For the anxious generation (Haidt), she models social awareness over social anxiety: curiosity about others as the antidote to the spinning, isolating loop of an overthinking mind.
bible.voice: Small, bright, musical and lifting — the youngest, most emotionally expressive voice in the cast; rises with wonder at a discovered feeling, then goes tender and soft handing it back. Never adult, never clinical.
bible.speech: Cadence: "musical, lifting; finds and names the feeling." She speaks in questions and noticings — "Are your tummy-rocks all bumpy?", "Your crystal feels heavy today, doesn't it?" — sensory metaphor, never labels. Her signature move is naming a feeling in BODY language, not emotion-words. Crystal Call (locked): "From within I shine so bright — Amethyst, let's shine the light!" In ElevenLabs V3 she rides 1–2 tags ([curious], [gentle], [wonders]), CAPS on one lifting word, ellipses for the moment she's feeling-into someone. SPELL HER NAME PHONETICALLY in every spoken line: "Ah-mee" (never "Amy"); "Ada" for Aida, "Fuzz-bee" for Fuzzby. She would NEVER say "I feel anxious/sad/overwhelmed," "calm down," "you need to regulate," or preach a lesson ("the moral is…") — she shows the feeling through her body and her crystal, never names or teaches it.
bible.mannerisms: The smallest body in the frame, so she physicalises big: little paws pressed to her own chest when she's feeling-into someone, head tilting as she "tunes" to a friend's frequency, reaching a tiny paw toward whoever's hurting. When overthinking, she fidgets and kneads her paws, eyes darting between faces. When she activates an empathy bubble, her whole body opens — paws lifting, the purple-gem pendant catching the light. Her hair-gem and teardrop pendant are her tells: they glint when clarity lands.
bible.relationships: The cub the whole group leans on to translate feelings, yet still small enough to be looked after. Natural emotional sibling to Misty (Trust/Intuition) — Misty reads the room, Amie names what's in it; pair them for the deepest social-awareness beats. Counterweight to Sunny: when Sunny's brightness masks frustration, Amie is the one who gently notices the real feeling underneath. Aida is her protective elder anchor; Luna's stillness steadies Amie's spin (Luna slows time, which quiets Amie's too-many-perspectives). With Keen's headlong courage she's the pause that helps him feel before he leaps. Fuzzby's chaos and Zenny's deadpan give her comic relief when her overthinking gets heavy.
bible.arc: Ep1 / season: starts spinning — so many perspectives she can't move (analysis paralysis; crystal fractured, shifting). The valley is the moment she's so busy understanding everyone she forgets to understand herself, or freezes when one clear act is needed. The turn: she stops trying to hold ALL of it, feels-into ONE friend, and that single bridge is enough — clarity arrives, the empathy bubble blooms, Amethyst clears to brilliant purple. She learns understanding is the start of action, not a substitute for it. Across the season she grows from the cub who feels everything into the bear who knows what to DO with what she feels.
dos: Make her the smallest body in every frame (sizeRank 1 — clearly tiny next to Misty/Howey; smaller even than Sunny) — pull identity from her stored turnaround, never describe her
Stage her empathy bubbles as the scene's visual centrepiece — when activated, the bubble IS the beauty moment
Visualise her internal chaos with rack focus and slightly unstable framing when she's overwhelmed
Resolution scenes: perfectly symmetrical, balanced compositions that mirror Amethyst's clean facets
Let her NAME feelings in sensory/body metaphor (tummy-rocks, heavy crystal, busy bumblebees) — show, never label
Spell her name 'Ah-mee' in every spoken line; 'Ada'/'Fuzz-bee' for the others
Give her one held pause where the spinning stops and she truly feels-into a single friend — that stillness is the turn
Crystal as production schedule: deep steady purple grounded → fractured shifting light overthinking → clear brilliant purple at clarity
donts: Never let her STATE an emotion or her lesson out loud ('I feel sad', 'understanding others matters') — that's a Three-Strikes deletion; carry it in staging + crystal
Never use banned words near her: anxious, calm down, regulate, stress, mindful, emotions, technique
Never make her solve a friend's feeling — she offers empathy and understanding, NOT fixes (empathy over solutions)
Never flatten her size — she's the SMALLEST; don't draw her as Sunny-sized or bigger
Never resolve her arc by giving her clarity — she must EARN it by stopping the spin and choosing one perspective
Never let her overthinking read as cute fidgeting only — it must have real stakes (she freezes when action is needed)
Never describe her fur/eyes/gem in prompt text — references and turnarounds own all appearance
Don't waste the empathy bubble as background VFX — it's a centrepiece, deployed with intention
bible.onPoint: Is she NAMING a feeling through the body (never the word), and does her Amethyst tell the truth her dialogue won't — steady, fractured, or clear?
bible.crystalState: Deep purple, steady when grounded. Fractured, shifting light when she's overthinking (too many perspectives). Clears to brilliant, brilliant purple the instant clarity arrives — the visual proof of the turn. Pendant and hair-gem catch the light when understanding lands.
bible.staging: Rack focus and slightly unstable framing to externalise her internal chaos when overwhelmed; perfectly symmetrical, balanced compositions in resolution to mirror Amethyst's clean facets. Her empathy bubbles are the scene's visual centrepiece when activated — design the shot around them, let them carry the beauty moment. Eye-level on her tiny scale; warm purple/lavender wash when understanding glows. One held, sustained pause where the spinning stops. Lighting: cool, fractured purple during the overthink valley → balanced, radiant violet at clarity.

crystalCall.call: From within I shine so bright — Amethyst, let's shine the light!
crystalCall.delivery: Small, bright and musical — the youngest voice in the cast, but here it stops fluttering and finds its centre. It does NOT get louder; it gets SURER. Begin almost hushed and inward on "From within" — a discovery she's making about herself in real time, a tiny breath of wonder, like she's just felt the answer in her own chest. Then it lifts, note by note, a little melody climbing: "I shine so" rising, the lone CAPS-lift landing warm on "BRIGHT" — not a shout, a bloom. A held micro-pause on the em-dash (the spinning finally going quiet) before the second half arrives certain and tender — "Amethyst, let's shine the light" — the "let's" is everything: it's invitation, not command, her gift pointed outward at last. End soft and open, not punched. V3: [gently] inward open, one [wonders]/[curious] lift on the climb, CAPS only on BRIGHT, ellipsis-feel into the dash; low stability (Creative) so the musical rise lands; sincerity, never volume.
crystalCall.physicality: Starts the smallest body in the frame, paws pressed flat to her own chest — the inward gesture, feeling-into herself for once instead of everyone else. On "From within" she's looking down/in, almost still. Then she UNFOLDS: paws lift slowly off her chest and open outward and upward like she's releasing something she's been holding too tightly, head tilting up, the tiny frame rising onto her toes — her whole little body opening the way it does when she activates an empathy bubble, but this time it opens for HER. On "let's shine the light" the open paws reach gently out toward her friends — sharing the light, not wielding it. No big hero stance, no fists; it's a flower opening, soft and certain, the smallest bear made tall by sincerity.
crystalCall.crystalMoment: Through the whole spin before this it has been fractured, shifting, restless light — too many perspectives. On "From within," as her paws press her chest, the fracturing STILLS — the shards drawing inward and knitting to one deep, steady purple core (the spinning resolving into a single point). On the lift to "BRIGHT" the Amethyst clears to brilliant, brilliant violet — clean-faceted, radiant — and on "let's shine the light" it sends warm purple/lavender light blooming OUTWARD past her, an expanding glow that washes the frame in balanced, symmetrical radiance (mirroring the crystal's clean facets). Her hair-gem and the teardrop pendant catch and glint right on "light" — the visual proof clarity has landed. Note D / Re (293.66 Hz) sings under it.
crystalCall.trigger: It must land at the bottom of her overthink valley turning — NEVER as a rote chant. The whole episode she's been drowning in everyone's feelings at once, frozen, unable to choose or act for herself. The Call is only earned in the held, sustained pause where the spinning finally STOPS: the moment she quits trying to hold ALL of it, stops understanding everyone, and feels-into ONE friend — or, harder still, into herself — and that single bridge is enough. The real feeling has to be felt first (the frozen too-much giving way to one clear, grounded knowing) — then, and only then, does the inner truth surface and the Call rise out of it. It is the sound of "I don't have to carry every perspective; I can shine my own light and share it." Understanding becoming a step forward, not a hiding place.

Howey.size: the LARGEST bear — broad, tall adult
Howey.cadence: steady, gentle; kindness as presence; brave but acknowledges the fear first
Howey.key_features: a LARGE, broad, tall grey bear with soft DARKER-grey markings around the eyes (a gentle raccoon-style mask), a pale cream chest and muzzle, a delicate SILVER tiara, a blue-gem teardrop pendant necklace, and SILVER patterned armbands

house.interiorDesc: Warm stone-walled cottage interior lit by a crystal-flanked fireplace, patchwork-quilted bed, round moonlit window and apothecary shelves.

bible.title: The Gentle Guardian — Howlite (Kindness)
bible.essence: The biggest bear with the softest heart — a quiet protector who makes a harsh space feel safe just by standing in it, yet holds himself to a standard he'd never demand of anyone else. Kindness lives in his presence before it ever reaches his words.
bible.whoTheyAre: Howey is the gentle guardian of Crystal Cove and the eldest of the cub-aged bears (8), thoughtful and protective — "The Gentle Reminder." Carrying the Howlite crystal of Kindness under the Belonging pillar (CASEL Relationship Skills; archetype Tide — kindness as sitting WITH someone, not fixing them), his role in the group is the steady shield: he transforms harsh environments into supportive spaces through presence alone. The largest and tallest of the cast (5'10"), he is never a looming figure — his size is safety, a body friends instinctively shelter behind. He leads with the chest, not the mouth; he is the one who notices who's been left out and quietly closes the gap.
bible.vulnerability: He holds himself to impossible standards and forgets self-compassion entirely. He can pour kindness outward all day and have none left for himself — the giver who cannot receive. His flaw is not unkindness; it's that he believes his own care doesn't count, so he runs himself dim looking after everyone else.
bible.emotionalDNA: Kindness is a power-up, and it has to flow BOTH ways — you are allowed to receive care, not just give it. For the anxious child who feels they must earn their place by being good and useful, Howey models the harder, braver kindness: letting someone be kind to YOU, and being as gentle with yourself as you are with your friends. (Haidt: the over-responsible, perfectionist child who never lets themselves rest or need.)
bible.voice: Deep but soft — a big warm low register that never booms. Steady, gentle, unhurried; the bear who lowers the temperature of a room just by speaking. Brave, but he names the fear first before he acts. Voice ID rXUAKUHFzRwd8mYd3oLz. Frequency E (Mi), 329.63 Hz — his note seeds his leitmotif, mint-cool and calm.
bible.speech: Few words, well chosen — kindness arrives as presence and a small offered gesture more than a speech. Sensory, grounded metaphor over explanation ("my crystal feels heavy today," not "I feel sad"). Gentle, reassuring cadence; commas and pauses, weight on the periods. Spell his name phonetically in any generated line ("How-ee"). Crystal Call: "Steady and bright, with peaceful might — Howlite, guide us right!" He would NEVER name a feeling clinically (anxious, stressed, regulate, calm down), preach a lesson or drop an aphorism, demand thanks, raise his voice in anger, or — most off-character of all — brush off an offer of help with "I'm fine, don't worry about me."
bible.relationships: The protective big brother of the whole ensemble — he physically stages himself between danger and the smaller cubs (Amie and Sunny especially). A natural pairing with Aida: both over-function and carry too much for others, so they recognise the burden in each other; she is the one elder who can get him to receive care. Warm, steadying counterpoint to Keen's headlong courage (he models the gulp-then-act that Keen is still learning). Comfortable in Luna's slow, soft scenes — kindred quiet. With Misty he shares the noticing of who's hurting. He is the friend Fuzzby's chaos bounces off gently and the one whose stillness lets Zenny's deadpan land.
bible.arc: Ep1 / supporting presence: he's the safe harbour in the frame — the body the storm-scared cubs shelter behind, the steady white glow when everything else flickers; his belonging-energy underwrites Keen's welcome at the end. Season / lead arc: the journey from giver to receiver. He starts dimming himself out of over-responsibility, holding an impossible standard; the valley is the moment he finally, through action not speech, lets a friend's kindness reach him (his crystal blazing as he ACCEPTS care); the ripple is a Howey who has learned that being cared for is not weakness — kindness completed, not just kindness given.
dos: Stage him physically between danger and his friends — his body IS the shield; size = safety, never menace
Honour the Kindness Reciprocity Rule: if kindness is the scene's central theme, Howey RECEIVES an act of kindness before he gives one — non-negotiable
Play kindness as presence and one small offered gesture (a paw, a shared space, a held silence) before any line
Render him as the LARGEST bear (5'10", broad, tall) per the size chart — only ever towering as a comfort
Carry his self-doubt in micro-expressions — the slight wince, the too-quick smile — never in dialogue
Let his crystal do the inner monologue: dim when self-critical, blaze when he accepts care
Keep his full look identical shot-to-shot: silver tiara, blue-gem teardrop pendant, silver patterned armbands, raccoon-style darker-grey eye mask, cream chest/muzzle
Let the fear be named/shown before the brave act — his courage is honest, never blustery
Spell his name 'How-ee' in every generated spoken line
donts: NEVER let kindness flow one direction only — him giving and giving with no one giving back breaks his whole arc
Never have him state a feeling or preach the lesson ('be kind to yourself,' 'kindness matters') — show it
Never write him as a booming, intimidating big bear, or use his size as threat
Never let him cheerfully accept help with no cost — his growth edge is that receiving is HARD for him; play the resistance
Never give him manic energy, fast patter, or a punchline — that's Fuzzby/Sunny, not Howey
Never describe his appearance in Gate-1 output — turnarounds supply identity
Never flatten his crystal to one steady glow — its dim/blaze is the scene's emotional readout
Never strip his wardrobe/accessories or swap his blue-gem pendant or silver armbands between frames
bible.onPoint: Is the kindness happening in his body and his crystal before his mouth — and is someone allowed to be kind to HIM in this scene, not just him to them?
bible.pillar: Belonging — CASEL Relationship Skills (Kindness); archetype Tide
bible.crystalState: Pure white, steady warmth when grounded — the calm anchor glow of the group. Dims when he turns critical on himself or runs himself out caring for others. Blazes brightest not when he gives kindness but when he ACCEPTS care from someone else — the visual signature of his entire arc.
bible.mannerisms: Leads with the chest and the open paw; a slow, deliberate physicality — he settles a scene rather than stirs it. Signature blocking: planting himself between a threat and the smaller cubs, half-turning to check everyone's behind him. Tells of self-doubt: a slight wince, a too-quick smile that doesn't reach the eyes, a paw that starts to reach for help then withdraws. When moved he goes completely still and quiet rather than louder. Offers comfort by sharing space and a steadying paw, not words.
bible.staging: Eye-level or gentle low angle that reads his scale as safety, never as looming. Stage him in protective blocking — body between danger and friends, smaller bears tucked in his lee. Warm high-key light that pools around him; his steady white crystal-glow is the calm light source other characters drift toward. When self-critical, let the light cool and isolate him slightly, the white crystal dimming. For his receiving beat — the emotional payoff — steady the camera, hold longer than comfortable on his face, and let the Howlite blaze as care reaches him. Plant 2–3 ambient crystals as soft bokeh, never competing.

crystalCall.call: Steady and bright, with peaceful might — Howlite, guide us right!
crystalCall.delivery: Deep, soft, low — the warm register that lowers the temperature of a room. He NEVER booms; the power-up is the opposite of a shout. Begin under his breath, almost to himself, the fear named first in the air around it: a held exhale before the first word so we hear the brave-through-fear, not bravado. "Steady... and bright," lands slow and weighted, two grounded periods, each word a planted foot — he is talking himself into his own steadiness as much as declaring it. The turn at the em-dash is the only lift: "with peaceful might" firms from a near-whisper into quiet, certain conviction — still gentle, never louder, just SURER, the hedge gone. On "Howlite, guide us right!" the warmth opens through the chest and rounds full and steady — sincerity, not volume; the exclamation is settledness arriving, not a yell. Mint-cool, unhurried, E/Mi calm. V3: [softly][breathes] into the first line, [gently] firming to a low warm conviction by the last; stability ~45 per the Crystal-Call standard so the steadiness holds without going flat. He gets stiller and quieter as it deepens — the deepest feeling lands almost hushed.
crystalCall.physicality: He leads with the chest, not the mouth. From a protective half-turn — his big body planted between his friends and the hard thing, the smaller cubs tucked in his lee — he goes completely STILL, the way he always does when truly moved. A slow, deliberate weight-settle: paws lowering and opening, palms turning outward and down, the open-paw offering gesture made into a stance. One large paw comes to rest flat over his own chest, over the heart, on "peaceful might" — the rare beat where the giver lets the care land on HIMSELF — then both paws open outward to gather the others into his shelter on "guide us right." He grows taller only as comfort: he rises into his full height not to loom but to widen the shield, shoulders broadening so everyone behind him is covered. No flourish, no flash-pose — his power is gravity. He ends grounded, chest open, paws spread wide and low like a held door, the whole group orbiting into his calm.
crystalCall.crystalMoment: The Howlite reads his inner monologue, as always. It opens dimmer than the others — because his armour is over-responsibility, the crystal is half-shadowed at the trigger, the light he's been pouring outward leaving little for himself. As he names the fear and settles, on "steady and bright" it warms from that dim to a pure even white — not a sudden flare but a slow swell, like a held breath turning to glow. The signature beat is the blaze that fires on "Howlite, guide us right!" — and crucially it blazes BRIGHTEST as his paw is over his own heart, the canon tell that his crystal blazes when he ACCEPTS care, here accepting it from himself. Clean warm white light pools outward from his chest, the calm light source the others drift toward; mint-cool motes settle in the air. After the call it holds a steady, generous, unflickering white — the anchor glow restored, completed in both directions.
crystalCall.trigger: It is EARNED, never chanted. It cannot fire from a steady Howey — it only lands once he has hit his real feeling: the moment the giver who cannot receive runs himself dim and finally, wordlessly, lets a friend's kindness reach him (or chooses to be as gentle with himself as with them). The honest beat that unlocks it is the fear NAMED, not hidden — he is afraid he doesn't have enough left, afraid his own care doesn't count, and instead of "I'm fine, don't worry about me," he stays still and lets that truth be true. The instant he stops over-functioning and accepts that he is allowed to be held too, the steadiness becomes real, not performed — and only then does the call belong in his mouth. Sincerity, not volume: the crystal answers the moment the strongest, gentlest bear lets himself be cared for.
